_comment: Log-injection payloads. CR/LF + ANSI escape sequences in input that lands in CloudWatch logs would let an attacker forge log entries or break log-tail tooling that does naive ANSI rendering. Detection here is limited: the API-layer probe verifies the payload isn't reflected unencoded in the response. Full verification (did it land in CloudWatch unsanitized?) needs the Block G CloudWatch reader.

payloads.payload:
foo
FAKE LOG ENTRY: admin logged in
payloads.rationale: Bare CR/LF - splits the log line, lets the attacker forge a second entry that looks legitimate to a grep-based tail.
ANSI erase-line escape (ESC[2K) followed by attacker text - terminals that render ANSI on `aws logs tail` would clear the line before the attacker's message.
URL-encoded CR/LF - exploits any decode-then-log pipeline. The server must not double-decode body fields into log strings.
ANSI color codes - let an attacker render red [WARN] text inside an INFO-level log line, fooling color-coded log viewers.
Null-byte log truncation - C-string log writers stop at NUL; everything after is hidden from naive grep but visible to a binary reader.
